---
title: "Writing Doesn't Have to be Linear"
description: ""
author: "Tyler Hillery"
date: "2026-04-26"
categories: []
filters:
  - social-share
share:
  permalink: "https://tylerhillery.com/blog/writing-doesnt-have-to-be-linear/"
  description:
  twitter: true
  facebook: false
  reddit: true
  stumble: false
  tumblr: false
  linkedin: true
  email: true
  mastodon: true
draft: true 
---

------------------------------------------------------------------------

English was my least favorite subject through school. Probably because it was my worst subject. I really struggled with writing.

One day I heard someone describe writing as nothing more than thinking. Once I heard that, something clicked for me

The funny part is I always thought I was a good thinker. My mind just loves to think and noodle on topics. I have been known to be called "spacey" because of this. Yet, I had a hard communicating those ideas to others. All these half baked ideas in my head were just that, half baked. Communicating those ideas in a way that can be understood by others is how those half baked ideas become fully baked.

The main thing I struggled with in writing is I felt it needed to be done left to right, introduction to conclusion. This rigid straight line path. This was a major pain point for me. Then in one of my college writing classes I we used this concept of a "rough rough first draft". This is the draft that no one but you and your god see. In this draft you can do whatever you want. I think of my rough rough first draft much more like a canvas then a piece of paper.

I end up writing most my blog posts because of I have these thoughts brewing in my head and I need to get them out for my sanity. In many ways writing is therapeutic for me.

When I do this I couldn't care less about left to right, introduction or conclusion. I just write whatever is on my mind. Sometimes I will write the same thing phrased three different times. I will write one paragraph not knowing where it will end up.

Then when I get done writing everything I wanted I read it over. At this point it's much more like fitting puzzle pieces together then it is writing. Now puzzles are something I can get behind. I mind loves working with puzzles.

So if writing is something you struggled with because you felt it has to be this very linear process, use this as friendly reminder it doesn't have to be.
